problem_statement: Freelance graphic designers struggle with payment security and scope creep, while clients fear poor quality work. There is no centralized platform that offers escrow payments, standardized portfolios, and dispute resolution specifically for this niche.
business_objectives: Launch a secure marketplace for freelance graphic designers.
Achieve 10,000 active users within the first 6 months.
Reduce payment disputes by 50% compared to industry average.
Generate revenue through a 10% commission on completed projects.
project_scope_in_scope: User registration and profile management for Designers and Clients.
project_scope_out_of_scope: Integration with third-party project management tools.
functional_requirements: System must allow users to sign up via email or social media.
System must process payments via Stripe Connect.
System must hold funds in escrow until project approval.
System must allow designers to upload portfolio items (images/videos).
System must provide a chat interface for project communication.
non_functional_requirements: Page load time must be under 2 seconds.
System must comply with GDPR and PCI-DSS.
Data must be encrypted at rest and in transit.
data_requirements: Store user profiles including portfolio data.
Store transaction history and payment status.
Store messaging logs for dispute resolution.
Track project status (Open, In Progress, Completed, Disputed).
assumptions: Stripe Connect will be the primary payment gateway.
Users will have valid email addresses.
AWS S3 will be used for file storage.
risks: Regulatory changes in gig economy laws.

missing_requirements: Review and rating system for designers and clients.
Search and filtering mechanism for finding projects/designers.
Admin dashboard for user and content management.
clarification_questions: What are the specific criteria for 'Portfolio Reviews' mentioned in the idea?
How should the dispute resolution process be structured? Is it manual or semi-automated?
Are there specific file types or size limits for portfolio uploads?
risk_flags: Reliance on a single payment provider (Stripe).
Potential for off-platform transactions to avoid fees.
Handling of intellectual property rights for design assets.

recommended_encryption: AES-256 for all database fields marked 'Confidential' or 'Restricted'.
TLS 1.3 for all data in transit.
retention_policy_suggestions: Chat history: 2 years (for dispute resolution evidence).
access_control_recommendations: Restrict database access to specific IP ranges (allowlist).
Require Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) for Admin console access.

microservices.name: Auth Service
microservices.description: Handles OIDC authentication and session management

microservices.name: Project Core
microservices.description: Manages portfolio listings and search index

microservices.name: Escrow Engine
microservices.description: Payment logic and dispute state machine
